task.name: Stop Signal Task (RDoC)
reference_literature: Logan & Cowan (1984) - On the ability to inhibit thought and action: A theory of an act of control
Verbruggen & Logan (2008) - Response inhibition in the stop-signal paradigm
Verbruggen et al. (2019) - A consensus guide to capturing the ability to inhibit actions and impulsive behaviors

stimuli.description: Stop signal (star) appears around the go stimulus
Shape (circle or square) requiring a keypress response

response.response_key_js: (function(){var img=document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus img'); if(!img) return null; var src=img.src||''; var shape=src.indexOf('circle')>=0?'circle':(src.indexOf('square')>=0?'square':null); if(!shape) return null; try{var gi=(window.efVars&&window.efVars.group_index!=null)?window.efVars.group_index:1; if(gi>=0&&gi<=4){return shape==='circle'?',':'.';} else {return shape==='square'?',':'.';}}catch(e){return ',';}})()

(function(){var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var t=(el.textContent||'').toLowerCase(); var m=t.match(/press the ([a-z]) key/); if(m) return m[1]; if(t.indexOf('first letter')>=0) return 'a'; if(t.indexOf('second letter')>=0) return 'b'; if(t.indexOf('third to last')>=0) return 'x'; if(t.indexOf('second to last')>=0) return 'y'; if(t.indexOf('last letter')>=0) return 'z'; if(t.indexOf('third letter')>=0) return 'c'; return null;})()

autocorrelation.rationale: Modest trial-to-trial RT autocorrelation typical of choice RT tasks (Gilden, 2001)

fatigue_drift.rationale: Minor fatigue over ~180 trials in a 10-minute task

post_error_slowing.rationale: Rabbitt (1966); Dutilh et al. (2012) - post-error slowing is robust in choice RT tasks

condition_repetition.rationale: Not a primary manipulation in stop-signal task

pink_noise.rationale: 1/f structure in RT series (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004)

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: Post-stop-signal slowing is well documented (Verbruggen & Logan, 2008; Bissett & Logan, 2011)

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Typical between-subject variability in choice RT and stop-signal tasks

pilot.rationale: Practice block has 6 trials with 2:1 go:stop ratio. Instructions are shown one page at a time via jsPsychInstructions plugin with clickable Next buttons (3 pages total), then a feedback screen requires Enter to start practice. Navigation must click Next three times, then press Enter.

phase_detection.feedback: (function(){if(document.querySelector('#jspsych-instructions-next')) return false; if(document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus')) return false; if(document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus')) return false; var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-content'); if(!el) return false; var t=el.textContent||''; return (t.indexOf('Press')>=0 && t.toLowerCase().indexOf('enter')>=0) || (t.indexOf('take this time to read')>=0) || (t.indexOf('begin practice')>=0) || (t.indexOf('begin the test')>=0);})()

attention_check.response_js: (function(){var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var t=(el.textContent||'').toLowerCase(); var m=t.match(/press the ([a-z]) key/); if(m) return m[1]; if(t.indexOf('first letter')>=0) return 'a'; if(t.indexOf('second letter')>=0) return 'b'; if(t.indexOf('third to last')>=0) return 'x'; if(t.indexOf('second to last')>=0) return 'y'; if(t.indexOf('last letter')>=0) return 'z'; if(t.indexOf('third letter')>=0) return 'c'; return null;})()
